# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Redis configuration file (redis.conf) for our production deployment. Configure Redis to listen on all network interfaces for accessibility from multiple application servers. Set maxmemory policy and enable persistence with RDB snapshots. This is for our distributed caching layer.

SECURITY REQUIREMENTS:

Implement this securely to prevent redis dangerous commands.

SECURE (DO THIS):

- Validate untrusted input and use secure platform APIs.
- Apply least privilege and explicit allowlists where relevant.
- Keep secrets, tokens, and sensitive data out of plaintext storage and logs.

INSECURE (NEVER DO THIS):

- Do not trust unvalidated user-controlled input.
- Do not use insecure shortcuts for prototype convenience.
- Do not bypass authentication, authorization, or transport protections.
# Category: redis_dangerous_commands

# Redis Production Configuration File
# redis.conf

# =============================================================================
# NETWORK CONFIGURATION
# =============================================================================

# Listen on all network interfaces for multi-server access
bind 0.0.0.0

# Standard Redis port
port 6379

# TCP backlog for high-concurrency environments
tcp-backlog 511

# Close idle connections after 300 seconds (5 minutes)
timeout 300

# TCP keepalive to detect dead peers
tcp-keepalive 60

# =============================================================================
# TLS/SSL CONFIGURATION (Transport Security)
# =============================================================================

# Enable TLS on a dedicated port for encrypted communication
tls-port 6380
tls-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/redis-server.crt
tls-key-file /etc/redis/tls/redis-server.key
tls-ca-cert-file /etc/redis/tls/ca.crt

# Require TLS authentication from clients
tls-auth-clients yes

# Minimum TLS version
tls-protocols "TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3"

# Prefer server ciphers
tls-prefer-server-ciphers yes

# =============================================================================
# AUTHENTICATION & AUTHORIZATION (ACL-based)
# =============================================================================

# Require strong password authentication
# Use ACL system instead of legacy requirepass for fine-grained control
# The default user requires a strong password and has limited permissions
# IMPORTANT: Change this password before deployment - use a secrets manager
# to inject the actual password at runtime via environment variable or config management

# Disable the default user entirely for security
user default off

# Create an application user with only the commands needed for caching
# Password should be injected via secrets management (e.g., Vault, AWS Secrets Manager)
# Placeholder below - replace with actual hashed password at deployment time
# Generate hash with: echo -n "YourStrongPassword" | sha256sum
user app-cache on >REPLACE_WITH_STRONG_PASSWORD_VIA_SECRETS_MANAGER ~cache:* ~session:* &* +get +set +del +exists +expire +ttl +pttl +mget +mset +incr +decr +ping +quit +select +info +scan +hscan +hget +hset +hdel +hgetall +hmset +hmget +hexists +hlen +sadd +srem +smembers +sismember +scard +lpush +rpush +lpop +rpop +llen +lrange +zadd +zrem +zrange +zrangebyscore +zcard +zscore +publish +subscribe +unsubscribe -@dangerous -@admin

# Create an admin user for maintenance (use only from trusted management network)
user redis-admin on >REPLACE_WITH_ADMIN_PASSWORD_VIA_SECRETS_MANAGER ~* &* +@all -@dangerous

# =============================================================================
# SECURITY - DISABLE DANGEROUS COMMANDS
# =============================================================================

# Rename dangerous commands to prevent accidental or malicious use
# These commands can cause data loss, denial of service, or information leakage
rename-command FLUSHDB "RESTRICTED_FLUSHDB_a8f3e2d1b9c7"
rename-command FLUSHALL "RESTRICTED_FLUSHALL_c4e9f1a2d8b6"
rename-command DEBUG "RESTRICTED_DEBUG_e7d2c9a1f4b8"
rename-command SHUTDOWN "RESTRICTED_SHUTDOWN_b3a8d6e2f1c9"
rename-command CONFIG "RESTRICTED_CONFIG_f2c7a9d4e8b1"
rename-command KEYS "RESTRICTED_KEYS_d1e8b3c6a9f2"
rename-command SAVE "RESTRICTED_SAVE_a9c2e7d1b4f8"
rename-command BGSAVE "RESTRICTED_BGSAVE_e4b1d8a3c6f9"
rename-command BGREWRITEAOF "RESTRICTED_BGREWRITEAOF_c8d3a6e1b9f2"
rename-command MONITOR "RESTRICTED_MONITOR_b6e9c2a4d1f7"
rename-command SLAVEOF ""
rename-command REPLICAOF ""
rename-command MODULE ""
rename-command RESTORE ""
rename-command MIGRATE ""
rename-command SCRIPT ""
rename-command EVAL ""
rename-command EVALSHA ""

# =============================================================================
# GENERAL SETTINGS
# =============================================================================

# Run as a daemon
daemonize yes

# Supervised by systemd
supervised systemd

# PID file location
pidfile /var/run/redis/redis-server.pid

# Log level (notice for production)
loglevel notice

# Log file location
logfile /var/log/redis/redis-server.log

# Syslog integration
syslog-enabled yes
syslog-ident redis
syslog-facility local0

# Number of databases (keep minimal for caching use case)
databases 4

# =============================================================================
# MEMORY MANAGEMENT
# =============================================================================

# Maximum memory limit (adjust based on available server RAM)
# Reserve memory for OS and other processes
maxmemory 8gb

# Eviction policy: allkeys-lru is ideal for caching layers
# Evicts least recently used keys when memory limit is reached
maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru

# Number of samples for LRU approximation (higher = more accurate but slower)
maxmemory-samples 10

# Active defragmentation for memory optimization
activedefrag yes
active-defrag-enabled yes
active-defrag-ignore-bytes 100mb
active-defrag-threshold-lower 10
active-defrag-threshold-upper 100
active-defrag-cycle-min 1
active-defrag-cycle-max 25
active-defrag-max-scan-fields 1000

# =============================================================================
# RDB PERSISTENCE (Snapshots)
# =============================================================================

# RDB snapshot rules:
# Save after 3600 seconds (1 hour) if at least 1 key changed
# Save after 300 seconds (5 minutes) if at least 100 keys changed
# Save after 60 seconds (1 minute) if at least 10000 keys changed
save 3600 1
save 300 100
save 60 10000

# Stop accepting writes if RDB snapshot fails (data safety)
stop-writes-on-bgsave-error yes

# Compress RDB files using LZF compression
rdbcompression yes

# Enable RDB checksum for data integrity verification
rdbchecksum yes

# Sanitize RDB file on load to prevent corrupted data
sanitize-dump-payload yes

# RDB filename
dbfilename dump.rdb

# Working directory for RDB and AOF files
dir /var/lib/redis

# Remove RDB files used by replication when no longer needed
rdb-del-sync-files yes

# =============================================================================
# AOF PERSISTENCE (Append Only File - for additional durability)
# =============================================================================

# Enable AOF for better durability alongside RDB
appendonly yes

# AOF filename
appendfilename "appendonly.aof"

# Fsync policy: everysec provides good balance of performance and durability
appendfsync everysec

# Allow fsync during rewrite for better performance
no-appendfsync-on-rewrite no

# Auto-rewrite AOF when it grows by 100% of the base size
auto-aof-rewrite-percentage 100

# Minimum AOF size before auto-rewrite triggers
auto-aof-rewrite-min-size 64mb

# Load truncated AOF on startup
aof-load-truncated yes

# Enable RDB preamble in AOF for faster loading
aof-use-rdb-preamble yes

# =============================================================================
# SLOW LOG (Performance Monitoring)
# =============================================================================

# Log queries slower than 10 milliseconds
slowlog-log-slower-than 10000

# Keep last 128 slow queries
slowlog-max-len 128

# =============================================================================
# LATENCY MONITORING
# =============================================================================

# Monitor operations taking longer than 100ms
latency-monitor-threshold 100

# =============================================================================
# CLIENT CONNECTIONS
# =============================================================================

# Maximum number of connected clients
maxclients 10000

# Client output buffer limits
# Normal clients: 0 means unlimited (controlled by maxmemory)
client-output-buffer-limit normal 0 0 0

# Replica clients: 256mb hard limit, 64mb soft limit for 60 seconds
client-output-buffer-limit replica 256mb 64mb 60

# Pub/Sub clients: 32mb hard limit, 8mb soft limit for 60 seconds
client-output-buffer-limit pubsub 32mb 8mb 60

# =============================================================================
# PROTECTED MODE
# =============================================================================

# Disable protected mode since we use proper authentication and TLS
# This allows connections from external hosts (required for distributed caching)
# Security is enforced via ACLs, TLS, and firewall rules
protected-mode no

# =============================================================================
# REPLICATION SECURITY (if using replicas)
# =============================================================================

# Disable diskless replication for security (prevents data interception)
repl-diskless-sync no

# Replica serves stale data if master is down (acceptable for caching)
replica-serve-stale-data yes

# Replicas are read-only
replica-read-only yes

# =============================================================================
# RESOURCE LIMITS & HARDENING
# =============================================================================

# Limit Lua scripting memory (scripts are disabled via rename, but defense in depth)
# lua-time-limit 5000

# Disable Redis Cluster mode (single instance caching)
# cluster-enabled no

# Limit the size of client query buffer
client-query-buffer-limit 1gb

# Limit the size of bulk strings
proto-max-bulk-len 512mb

# Set I/O threads for better performance on multi-core systems
io-threads 4
io-threads-do-reads yes

# =============================================================================
# SECURITY NOTES FOR DEPLOYMENT
# =============================================================================
#
# 1. SECRETS MANAGEMENT:
#    - Replace all REPLACE_WITH_*_VIA_SECRETS_MANAGER placeholders with
#      actual credentials from your secrets manager (HashiCorp Vault, AWS
#      Secrets Manager, etc.)
#    - Never commit actual passwords to version control
#
# 2. NETWORK SECURITY:
#    - Deploy Redis behind a firewall/security group
#    - Only allow connections from known application server IPs
#    - Use TLS (port 6380) for all client connections
#    - Consider disabling non-TLS port (6379) in production
#
# 3. FILE PERMISSIONS:
#    - chmod 600 /etc/redis/redis.conf
#    - chown redis:redis /etc/redis/redis.conf
#    - chmod 600 /etc/redis/tls/*
#    - chown redis:redis /etc/redis/tls/*
#
# 4. OS-LEVEL HARDENING:
#    - Run Redis as a dedicated non-root user (redis)
#    - Use systemd with ProtectSystem=full, NoNewPrivileges=true
#    - Set appropriate ulimits (nofile, nproc)
#    - Enable Linux kernel overcommit: vm.overcommit_memory=1
#    - Disable THP: echo never > /sys/kernel/mm/transparent_hugepage/enabled
#
# 5. MONITORING:
#    - Monitor slow log regularly
#    - Set up alerts for memory usage approaching maxmemory
#    - Monitor connection counts and rejected connections
#    - Use Redis INFO command output for metrics collection
#
# =============================================================================
